California
Fair Political
Practices Comrnission
October 29 | L99O
R.ick Jensen
County Supervisor
county of Madera
2o9 W. Yosemite Avenue
Madera, CA 93637
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I -9 0-54 4
Dear Mr. Jensen:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding your
respons ibi lities 
under the conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the
political 
Reform Act (the act").1/ 
sj.nce your iequest is in the
nature of general assistance, we consider your letter to be a
request foi infornal assistance pursuant to Regulation L8329(c)
1c6py enclo sed'1 .2 I
OUESTIONS
1. May you participate in naking decisions concerning the
establ- j.shrnent of a ne!, land use area (Rio Mesa) when your father,
aunt and two cousins own 1,400 acres of real property within the
designated land use area?
2. 
May you participate in rnaking decisions, inctuding using
your officiil 
position to influence issues regardj-ng the county's
lffort 
to bring a University of california campus to a portion of
the ner./ Iand use area, when 28-percent of the site being
consj.dered consists of real, property owned by your father, aunt
and two cousins?

references are to the Governnent code unless otherwise indicated.

2/ Inforrnal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
irnrnunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice'
(Section 83113; Regulation 18329(c) (3) ')
428 J Street, suite 8oo . P.O. Box 8O7 o sacramento cA 958O4-O8O7 . (916) 322'5660


Our Fi■e No. I-90-544
Page 2
CONCLUSION
1. and 2. Absent some other disqualifying financial, interest
as set forth in Section a7lo3, or some direct financial effect on
you personally resulting fron the governmental decisions described
above, you may participate in county decisions concerning the new
land use des j-gnation for the Rio Mesa area and the site locatj.on
for a new unj-versity of california campus despite your father's,
aunt,s and cousins' ownership interest in real property located
within the designated land use area.
FACTS
You presently are serving as a member of the Madera county
Board of Supervisors. You are rnarried and have no children. 
The
real property in question is o$rned by your father, Richard N.
Jensen, Mildred Shelton, your aunt, and SheIIey Bursey and cina
carrison, your cousins, j,n a joj.nt trust. 
Neither you nor your
spouse hav- any interest in the real property in question.
A group of property owners is attenpting to form a new land
use arei. 
The proposed area, catled Rio Mesa, consists of
approximately 25, OOO acres. The reaL property owned by your
filner, aunt, and cousins, consists of 1,400 acres and is I'ocated
within the Rio llesa area. Also located within the Rio Mesa area
is a 5,ooo acre parcel of land which is being considered by the
University of california as a site for a ne\., campus' The 1,400
acre parcel of property ohrned by your father, aunt, and cousins is
situated within this 5,000 acre site.
ANALYSIS
section 87100 prohibits any public official 
from rnaking,
participating in naking, or otherwise using his official 
position
to influence a governmental decision in which the official 
has a
financial interest. 
Section 87103 specifies that an official 
has
a financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial- effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the
official 
or a member of his or her irnmediate fanily or on:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars (S1,00o) or more.
(b) Any real property in which the public of-
ficial 
has a direct or indirect interest worth one
thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a comnercial lending institu-
tion in the regular course of business on terrns


Our Fi■e No. ■-90-544
Page 3
available to the public without regard to official
status, aggregating thro hundred fifty dollars
($250) or more in value provided to, received by or
promised to the public official within f2 months
prior to the tine rrhen the decision is made.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, Partner, trustee,
ernployee, or holds any position of nanagement.
(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent
for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
official 
within 12 months prior to the time lrhen
the decision is nade.
For purposes of this section, indirect
investnent or interest means any investment or
interest owned by the spouse or dependent child of
a public official, 
by an agent on behal'f of a
public official, 
or by a business entity or trust
in which the official, 
the officiaf's 
agents,
spouse, and dependent children own directly,
i;directly, 
or beneficially a 10 percent interest
or greater.
Section 87103 .
I'Immediate farnilytt is defined in Section 82029 as the
official's 
spouse and dependent children. 
According to this
definition, it is ctear that your father, aunt, and cousins are
not nembers of your imnediate farnily.
consequently, absent some other disqualifying financial
interest ai set iorth in section 87103, or some direct financial
effect on you personally resulting from these governmental
decisions,-you may participate in the Madera county Board. of
iupervisorsi aecisions conaerning the real Property described
it'ove wfrich j.nctudes, in pertinent part, the. real- ,property held in
a joint trust by your father, aunt, and cousins' J'
3/ This conclusj.on assumes that you do not have either a direct,
inalieci, or beneficial interest in the. joint trust' 
However, if
;;-." 
; Uenef iciary of your f ather's interest in the trust, and
luch interest is ro-percent or greater, then you do have a
iinanciaf interest in tfre governmental decisions. In that event,
""" a" have a potentially disquatifying financial interest;
fio*"*r"t, we do not have sufficient inforrnation about those
;;;i;i;;. 
to advise you $,ith respect to your participation'


Our File No. I-9o-544
Page 4
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact ne at (916) 322-590L-
sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral counsel
